differential_diagnoses: Nutritional optic neuropathy (Vitamin A deficiency leading to xerophthalmic keratopathy and optic neuropathy)
Combined nutritional deficiency (Copper, Vitamin B12, and Vitamin C deficiency)
Genetic optic atrophy (OPA1 or Leber hereditary optic neuropathy)
next_steps: Obtain emergent serum levels of Vitamin A (retinol), Vitamin E, Vitamin D, Vitamin C, Vitamin B12, copper, zinc, and selenium
Perform a complete blood count, peripheral smear, and coagulation profile (to evaluate for hematologic signs of malnutrition/scurvy)
Immediate referral to pediatric nutrition and gastroenterology for management of severe selective eating and potential malabsorption
Dilated fundus examination and possible ERG (electroretinography) under sedation to evaluate retinal rod/cone function
Initiate high-dose vitamin supplementation (Vitamin A, Vitamin C, and balanced multivitamin) under medical supervision
Coordinate with social work and behavioral specialists to address severe food selectivity and autism-related dietary restrictions
